paragraphs: The black mulberry (Morus nigra) is native to south-western Asia and has been cultivated so long its exact wild range is unknown. Its fruit ripens to a dark purple, almost black, and is richly flavoured, with an intense sweetness balanced by a pleasant tartness that is generally regarded as the best tasting of the mulberries.
The berries are relatively large and juicy and stain readily, so they are usually eaten fresh or made into jams, pies and cordials rather than shipped.

claims.text: Morus nigra is native to south-western Asia, long cultivated
Fruit is dark purple, almost black when ripe
Richly flavoured, sweet with a slightly acidic tang, considered the best-flavoured mulberry
